Concepts of Record & Reports
Definition of Record
The data, information, survey data which is collected by the Health care provider through in community & Health Hospitals is called as Record.
It includes vital statistics, immunizations of child & infant, immunizations of infant pregnant women, mortality, morbidity, fetal death, still birth, etc.
It Records the health care provider collects all these "informal" through the community.

authority.

# Purposes of Record & Reports
- Record & Report help the country to give accurate information.
- Record & Reports help the government to identify the no. of cases of disease.
- Record & Reports helps to make proper census accurate accurately.
- Record & Reports helps to tell about the incidence and prevalence of the disease in the particular area.
- Record & Reports helps to identify the spreading of disease in the area.
- Record & Reports helps to know about population of the country.
- Record & Reports tells & helps to know the vital statistics of country.
Legal Implications of Records & Reports-
The Legal Implications of Records-
→ Records should be collected by the approved Health care providers, A&M, AsHA or Anganwadi.
→ Records should be clear & concise in manner.
→ Records should be accurately and original.
→ Records should be applicable for the government.
→ Records will not be shown to everyone. It can only be shown to the higher authority where it get submitted.
Legal Implication of Reports -
→ The Reports should be approved by the Health care provider or the person who will be responsible for approving the data.
→ The Reports should not be in the undisciplined manner.
→ The Reports should be seen by anyone. Once the data has submitted on the online portal, it cannot be seen or implement by anyone.
→ The Reports should be in disciplined manner.
→ The Reports helps to identify the condition of that particular area, so it is important to be updated in disciplined manner to be in original form.
Principles of Record Writing
There are various principles of Record writing which are as-
- The Record should be neat and clean so that everybody on the higher authority can read clearly.
- The Record should be original. The Record should be collected by the people and should be collected accurate data.
- The Record should be collected by the Hospital (PHC, CHC and District Hospital).
- The vital statistics records should be collected by the Hospital or Municipal office to collect the data about Birth and death rate of individuals.
→ The Records should be collected by the person who are allotted to be collect the data.
→ The Records should be in the disciplined manner.
→ Records should be original, related to incidence & prevalence of disease in the particular area.
→ Record should be hidden & not should having everybody right to show & check as well.

The Nurse should be maintained the reports of the reports of the patient in the EHR.
The Nurse must be checked accurately to know about the Health status of the patient.
The Nurse should be maintained the every record of the patient including their bills, their disease, lab reports of the patients.
→ The proper uploading of the content help to Identify the disease incidence of prevalence.
→ To reference the patient in the territory or higher care unit, EMR helps to the patient.
→ For Neurele can evaluate the disease condition of the patient.
→ The Records, the proper Records helps to Identify the vital statistics, a census population & other related information.
RMNCI+A
→ RMNCI+A stands for.
→ Reproductive, Maternal, Neonates, Child Health and Adolescents.
→ The RMNCI+A program was valued under the National Health Ministry.
→ The RMNCI+A program was aimed to provide proper care of the Reproductive female, child neonates as well as adolescents.
→ In the late years, females were unaware about their health as well about their child. This was affected in the various complications of the mother as well as child neonates.
→ Still both state fetal death was increased rapidly. On that time the gov. take action to launch
the RMNCH program to take health.
## Aim 1
The aim of RMNCH & A is-
- To decrease the fetal mortality rate.
- To immunize the pregnant mother as well as newborn.
- To provide proper health care to the mother as well as their family.
- To aware the couple about the family planning.
- To educate the role of conception in the family planning.
- To provide proper diet during pregnancy.
- To provide proper diet after delivery.
To check the follow-ups of the mother.
To conduct the delivery at the hospital.
To provide the intervention of the father infant to first after the delivery.
To tell the importance of breast feeding first after the delivery.
To provide iron, folic acid & calcium tablets in the gestational period to reduce the risk of anemia.
To provide proper care to the mother during labour.
To educate about the adolescent girls about menes & the meniscal hygiene.
# Strategies
The Strategies of RMNCI+A
→ To provide nutritional diet to the pregnant women at the nearest Anganwadi Center.
→ To provide iron folic acid tablets + calcium tablets in the nearest Subcentre, CHE, & PHE.
→ To provide 84 hrs. delivery support system in PHE, CHE & district hospitals.
→ To educate the pregnant women by making meeting sessions in the Anganwadi centre or at the local area.
→ To provide proper care to the newborn, if it having any complicate after delivery.
→ To manage the transport system in the primary level so that
If there is complicated search
suddenly to the district hospital.
→ To proper provide proper health edes.
to the Anganwood, coworkers &
AHAs, so that they can educate
the pregnant lady in that
area.
→ To educate the Adolescent girls to
take proper diet, & maintain
proper hygiene in the menstruation
period.
Interventions across life stages -
Intervention across life stages
include-
→ During Birth
→ Childhood
→ Adolescents
→ Adulthood
→ Old age
1. During Birth-
Provide proper intervention to the
mother as well as the baby.
2. childhood -
Provide proper educat to the children & proper provide healthy diet because childhood is the age of growth & dev.
3. Adolescents -
The Adolescent age is very crucial for girls. They start their first meals & start puberty.
So, it is imp. to provide healthy & rich diet.
4. Adult -
Adult age also called as gestat Reproductive age varies from 18 - 99 yrs.
This is the age where women bear the pregnancy. So, the proper diet include iron is imp. for the mother, lack of blood cause various problems to the mother as well as fetus.
## Roles of Community Health Nurses in Strengthening Health System
Community Health Nurse is the very last stage of the Health Care delivery system, where she interact the people community people directly and knows the condition of the diseases.
It is the A+ community Health Nurse responsibility to tackle the things and disease and provide various Health Intervention to the community.
A Health system is still be good when the area is free of diseases.
Educate the community peoples about the various health disease which can suffer from the peoples, mosquitoes, durtyness, storage water, etc.
# Program Management
It is responsibility of the community health nurse to implement the program in the community which is run by the GOI.
- To provide info about the program & benefits of the program.
- To educate the community to take participate in the program.
- To complete the task of the community health nurse which is provided in the program.
- Educate ASTAS, ANIM & Anganwadi workers about the program, so that they can educate the community people by their own ways.
Monitoring to evaluate
It is responsibility of the Community Health worker to monitor or evaluate the progress of program which is planned by the Gov.
- Divide the work in the Animal & Aged & Anganwadi workers so that can not get confused related to their work.
- Give a accurate date or date in a month to submit the reports of the program.
- Provide proper education related to the program.
- Advice them to not make wrong work & make a record in the original or disciplined manner.

Segregation -
→ when the waste is collected, it get segregated by the segregation workers.
→ Every articles in the waste having their own process to be destroyed. So, it is imp. to segregate the waste.
→ Segregation is done mostly in the hospitals. But there is also
→ But before the final transportation, it is again checked to segregate the things.
Transportation -
→ The final step of Bio-medical waste management is to transport the waste to discard them from the far away from the population.
The transportation is done by large packed containers so that the smell or infectious particles could not effect the population.
After transportation, it get discarded by the various methods also depend upon the waste.
Red Cross
Red Cross people are used for cross society. One of the society, where the person, or soldier who gets disabled during the war, can carry disaster. They are called Red Cross.
Contribution
The Red Cross society is a
government funded society, in which they can manage their earnings.
In disaster management,
They provide shelter to the disaster affected peoples.
They also get contributed to the people who got injured during the disaster.
They also help the affected people and their family who lost their life.
Community Health
In the community, the Red Cross people can educate the peoples about the health program.
They help the Health workers to provide data about the area.
They can tell the popular about the National programs.
They contribute the health workers as well as the pop community.
Integrated Disease Surveillance
Profeet-
Integrated Disease surveillance
is as started in Nov. 2004 by the National Health Ministry.
Surveillance-
The surveillance means to keep eyes on the isolated data, disease or health condition or of dangerous diseases.
# Arms
- The aim of integrated disease surveillance project is
- To keep focus on the harmful diseases.
- To check the new cases of disease.
- To check the disease condition of the people in the population.
- To identify the vital statistics of the population.
- To evaluate the data from new cases on older cases.

Impact of popular control measures on maternal & child health-
This is various impact in popular control measure that are as-
Maternal = Proper Health.
- Blow scale of popular growth.
- Decrease scale of diseases related to reproductive system.
- Good hygiene.
- Economically stable.
- Physiological strong.
- Psychological stable.
- Prevention from various pelvic disease.
Child = Proper growth & Dev.
- Proper Nutrition.
- Proper education.
- Highten literacy rate.
- Employment ↑.
- Proper care with father as well as mother.
- Easy identification of disease.
To provide proper education of the child.
→ Increases the literacy rate.
→ Decreases the employment rate in the country.
→ Areas of proper shelter to everyone.
→ Secure jobs for everyone.
→ Low discrimination.
→ Good face.
→ Good development of child as well as mother.
→ Proper 4 nutritious diet to all.
Role of community Health Nurse in Mental Health D/O-
Identification-
- Identify the patient who has suffer from mental disability.
- Check their vitals, behaviour & contribution of society.
- Talk the patient to know about the severity of the disease.
Early detection-
- Not to leave the patient alone.
- Advise the family members to spent time with them.
- Provide full ambitious such diet.
- Explain the family members about their disease.
Management
- Not to leave the patient alone.
- Provide proper care to the patient & provide proper medical.
- Refer the patient if the treatment will not subside.
- Advice the patient family to not treat them like strangers.
- Provide proper treatment.
- Provide proper diet.
